title: Training
summary: MCAAT Admin checklist - Training. 18 unique items across 2 audiences.

source.title: MCAAT Admin Checklist - Training
source.publisher: Marine Corps Manpower and Reserve Affairs

items.question: Is the unit ensuring training data (PFT, CFT, rifle, schools, MCMAP etc.) are reported accurately within 5 days of the effective date?
Is the unit ensuring the NAVMC 11621 Body Composition Program (BCP) is properly completed IAW applicable regulations?
Is the unit ensuring BCP is reported within 5 days of the effective date?
Is the unit validating BCP dates are accurately reflected in the MCTFS for assignment, removal, or extensions that are processed by the unit?
For those BCP cases that have been recommended for administrative separation, has the unit initiated separation proceeding?
In those BCP cases where the command has determined the BCP assignment to be erroneous, has the command taken steps to clear the affected Marine's record?
Is the command ensuring key supporting documents to validate BCP periods are maintained on file for audit, and in accordance with retention policies?
Is the unit identifying Marines that are missing or have late training data (PFT, CFT, rifle, school completion, MCMAP) within the MCTFS?

Is the unit ensuring key supporting documents to validate training data entered in the MCTFS for training events conducted by the unit are maintained on file for audit, and in accordance with retention policies?
Is the command ensuring key supporting documents to validate BCP periods are maintained on file for audit, and in accordance with retention policies?
Is the unit identifying Marines that are missing or have late training data (PFT, CFT, rifle, school completion, MCMAP) within the MCTFS?
Is the unit ensuring training data (PFT, CFT, rifle, schools, MCMAP etc.) are reported accurately within 5 days of the effective date?

Is the unit ensuring key supporting documents to validate training data entered in the MCTFS for training events conducted by the unit are maintained on file for audit, and in accordance with retention policies?
Is the unit ensuring the NAVMC 11621 Body Composition Program (BCP) is properly completed IAW applicable regulations?
Is the unit ensuring BCP is reported within 5 days of the effective date?
Is the unit validating BCP dates are accurately reflected in the MCTFS for assignment, removal, or extensions that are processed by the unit?
For those BCP cases that have been recommended for administrative separation, has the unit initiated separation proceeding?
In those BCP cases where the command has determined the BCP assignment to be erroneous, has the command taken steps to clear the affected Marine's record?
